What it is (plain language): The private BGP AS number the NSX Edges use; announced to the top-of-rack switches
AS number of the first top-of-rack switch the Edges peer with
AS number of the second ToR (same as ToR-A if the fabric is one iBGP domain, else distinct)
VLAN carrying the Edge-to-ToR-A uplink network
VLAN carrying the Edge-to-ToR-B uplink network
Edge node 1 interface IP on uplink 1 (peers with ToR-A)
Edge node 1 interface IP on uplink 2 (peers with ToR-B)
Edge node 2 interface IP on uplink 1 (peers with ToR-A)
Edge node 2 interface IP on uplink 2 (peers with ToR-B)
The BGP neighbor address on ToR-A that the Edges connect to (usually the uplink-01 gateway)
The BGP neighbor address on ToR-B (usually the uplink-02 gateway)
Fast (sub-second) failure detection on the peerings - recommended
Equal-cost multipath across the Edge-to-ToR uplinks - required
OPTIONAL peer authentication - only fill in if BGP authentication is being used
Notes: Not required by NSX (neighbor IP + AS are the only required settings)

What it is (plain language): BGP timers - use the fabric standard, else the defaults
What the Edges announce to the fabric
What the fabric announces to NSX (typically just a default route)
Per-AZ ToR-A peering for the second availability zone
Per-AZ ToR-B peering for the second availability zone
